hypothesis: If the root-mean-square difference between conservative gradient-derived forces and directly predicted non-conservative forces (Force Delta, U∆) is large, then the true force error relative to DFT (εdirect) will be correspondingly large across equilibrium and adversarial out-of-distribution structures for diverse NNIP models, and U∆ will correlate with and predict εdirect at least as well as (and in OOD cases better than) ad-hoc ensemble variance without requiring access to training data.
